mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.5pp over 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: The joint growth of HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 50% of cases, or their correlation coefficient drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces inflationary pressures and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns during periods of monetary tightening.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO rises and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% triggers central bank rate hikes, creating headwinds for equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that support corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k new jobs.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP (GDPC1) following prior expansion signals economic weakness that depresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 declines by >1.0% qoq.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with declining capacity utilization (UNRATE) indicates overheating supply chains, creating inflationary risks that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of INDPRO > 105 and UNRATE < 5.0% fails to produce negative 20d returns in > 50% of cases, or their joint signal correlation drops below 0.3.
